clusters.title: Defence Secretary meets UN Assistant Secretary-General in New York

versions.title: Defence Secretary meets UN Assistant Secretary-General in New York
versions.deck: Continuing his official visit to the United Nations Headquarters in New York City, Sri Lanka s Secretary of Defence, Air Vice Marshal (Retd) Sampath Thuyacontha, engaged in a high-level meeting with Khaled Khiari, UN Assistant Secretary-General..MORE...

clusters.title: Colombo Archdiocese refutes reports on Cardinal Ranjith s resignation

clusters.title: CID complaint filed over cyberattack on Public Administration Ministry s website

clusters.title: Sri Lankan Rupee declines further as US Dollar exceeds Rs. 317

versions.title: Sri Lankan Rupee declines further as US Dollar exceeds Rs. 317

clusters.title: Former Minister Johnston Fernando granted bail

versions.title: Former Minister Johnston Fernando granted bail

versions.title: Sri Lanka probing $2.5 million cyber heist with international assistance � PM

clusters.title: Severe dry conditions drive rapid drop in Castlereagh and Maussakelle reservoir water levels

versions.title: Severe dry conditions drive rapid drop in Castlereagh and Maussakelle reservoir water levels
versions.deck: Water levels in the Castlereagh and Maussakelle reservoirs are declining rapidly due to the prevailing extreme dry weather conditions in the catchment areas on the western slopes of the Central Hills, engineers responsible for the reservoirs have reported. MORE..

versions.title: Two Bangladeshi nationals arrested with foreign cigarettes worth Rs. 4.26 mln at BIA

versions.title: Hanif Yusoof pitches Sri Lanka as regional investment hub at US corporate roundtable

clusters.title: 1Q exports edge up to $ 4.3 b, but March hit by Middle East tensions

versions.title: 1Q exports edge up to $ 4.3 b, but March hit by Middle East tensions

clusters.title: HIP sets new container throughput record with MSC vessel

clusters.title: Iran war to push over 30 m into poverty, UNDP warns

versions.title: Iran war to push over 30 m into poverty, UNDP warns
versions.deck: More than 30 million people could be pushed back into poverty due to the economic fallout from the Iran war, including disruptions to fuel and fertiliser supplies at a critical point in the agricultural cycle, United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Administrator Alexander De Croo said yesterday.

clusters.title: Sri Lanka’s first Online Safety Act case concludes at Colombo Chief Magistrate’s Court

versions.title: Sri Lanka’s first Online Safety Act case concludes at Colombo Chief Magistrate’s Court

clusters.title: $ 2.5 m debt breach hits during ‘vulnerable’ transition as Treasury reassures creditors

versions.title: $ 2.5 m debt breach hits during ‘vulnerable’ transition as Treasury reassures creditors

clusters.title: Harshana answers Harsha: No ‘technical default’ from lost $ 2.5 m credit payment

versions.title: Harshana answers Harsha: No ‘technical default’ from lost $ 2.5 m credit payment
versions.deck: Treasury Secretary Dr. Harshana Suriyapperuma yesterday said Sri Lanka does not face a technical default following the diversion of funds intended for Australia’s export credit agency, responding to concerns raised by Committee on Public Finance (CoPF) Chairman MP Dr. Harsha de Silva.

Thomas’ College, Mount Lavinia will be led by Number 8, Mckyle Karunaratne for the 2026 rugby season with high hopes of restoring their proud rugby legacy and bringing glory back to the school. clusters.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch

versions.title: Sri Lanka to see slow growth, widening fiscal deficit: Fitch
versions.deck: Fitch Ratings has said that Sri Lanka’s recovery remains fragile despite improved macroeconomic fundamentals, with the Middle East energy shock now testing the gains achieved through stabilisation and reforms. In its latest Sri Lanka update on credit development, Fitch said growth is expected to slow, inflation to rise and the fiscal deficit to widen in 2026, even as the economy builds on stronger fundamentals following the 2022 crisis.
